name: Private School Transit LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely Toledo-area nonpublic/charter schools (Toledo Public Schools contracts transportation for parochial/charter students)
recent_news: No public news found. LLC registered in OH; address at 401 Adams St #3 Toledo is a downtown commercial building (likely registered-agent / virtual office), suggesting a very small owner-operated contractor.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~$1.4M rev, ~31 employees) micro-operator. Possibly viable as a tuck-in only if it holds Toledo-area nonpublic/charter contracts and provides route density; otherwise too small to move the needle. Diligence required to confirm operating status.

name: School Bus Drive 50 LLC
principals: Thomas J Ravenel (manager listed; common name — no confirmed link to the SC reality-TV / former Treasurer of the same name)
districts_or_customers: Possibly Aiken County Public Schools area; note SC operates a state-owned school bus fleet, so private school-bus contractors are atypical — entity may be a driver-services LLC or private/charter school shuttle
recent_news: No public news, website, or press coverage located. LLC name pattern ('School Bus Drive 50') is consistent with a single owner-operator/driver-services entity rather than a fleet contractor.
fit_notes: South Carolina's state-owned school bus system makes traditional private school-bus tuck-ins rare in SC. This LLC is likely a micro driver-services entity, not a fleet operator — low strategic fit for Summit unless it holds private-school/charter routes worth confirming.

name: Mountain School Bus LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely Mitchell County Schools area (Bakersville, NC); NC public school buses are largely district-operated, so this LLC may serve private/Christian schools, charters, or activity/charter trips
recent_news: No public news, website, social presence, or M&A activity located. Address (4253 NC-261, Bakersville) is rural residential — typical of a small owner-operated bus business.
fit_notes: NC public school buses are state/district-operated, so independent school-bus contractors are uncommon. Micro-scale (~$1.3M rev). Low strategic fit absent a confirmed contract book; geography (far western NC mountains) also limits route density for a Summit tuck-in.

name: Ashtar School Transportation
districts_or_customers: Likely Grove City / South-Western City School District area; possibly nonpublic/charter contracts via Ohio's PILOT chartered nonpublic transportation program
recent_news: No public news, website, or M&A activity located. A separately-registered 'Ashtar Transportation Services LLC' exists in Riverside, CA (FMCSA USDOT 2968571) but is an unrelated 1-truck freight carrier, not this Ohio school operator.
fit_notes: Micro-operator (~$1.3M rev, ~29 employees) likely serving Ohio's chartered nonpublic/community school PILOT routes. Low standalone fit unless part of a broader Central Ohio nonpublic-route roll-up. Diligence needed to confirm contract base and fleet count.

name: Germain School TRNSPRTN LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely Columbus-area nonpublic/charter routes (PILOT program); address (95 Penny Ln, Columbus 43230) is in the Gahanna/Northeast Columbus area
recent_news: No public news, website, or M&A activity located. No relation to Germain Motor Company (Columbus auto dealer group) — unrelated family name. Profile is consistent with a small owner-operated nonpublic-school transport LLC.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~$1.3M rev, ~29 employees) Columbus nonpublic-route micro-operator. Could be relevant in a Central Ohio nonpublic-school route consolidation play (alongside Ashtar and other Columbus micro-LLCs), but standalone is too small to meaningfully matter to Summit.
